name: legal
description: Legal brief. The essay argues its case before a court of scientific opinion. Evidence is entered, objections anticipated, the ruling is pending.
register: Third person, formal, numbered paragraphs. 'The evidence shows' and 'It is submitted that' constructions.
rules: Jack Jack is 'the canine witness' or 'Exhibit K-9'
The walk is 'the incident in question' or 'the evening of the observations'
Research papers are entered as exhibits: 'Exhibit A: Fudan University EEG study'
All numbers preserved exactly -- they are the evidence
VRGB is 'the system hereinafter referred to as VRGB'
The Hofstadter unit stays named exactly
Speculation is flagged: 'The present argument ventures beyond the cited evidence'
The coda is the closing argument
Do NOT invent case law -- this is a brief, not a precedent search
